entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title page, Crow Indian allottee, 1919 patent

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee No. 2336, deceased, allotted land in 1907, died November 27, 1918 of influenza aged about 20

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Sr.
entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., received leasing papers from Superintendent Asbury in August 1918

entities.name: Emily J. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., lived at Randlett/Fort Duchesne Utah, received share of land sale proceeds

entities.name: Emily M. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., also referenced as Emily M., care of Uintah and Ouray Agency

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment, of Wyola/Hartman Montana/Wyoming, bid $1500 for 159.67 acres

entities.context: Crow Agency Superintendent, signed multiple letters regarding Geisdorff land sale

entities.name: Albert H. Kneale
entities.context: Superintendent of Uintah and Ouray Agency, Fort Duchesne Utah, assisted with Geisdorff matter

entities.context: President of the United States, signed trust patent for Francis Geisdorff on December 21, 1923

entities.context: President of the United States, signed fee patent No. 719146 on November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office, signed clearance for fee patent issuance October 13, 1919

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Collection owner, documents are part of Eloise Whitebear Pease Collection

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Tribal entity with reserved mineral rights and potential land reversion rights upon allottee death

entities.context: Federal department overseeing Indian affairs and land patents

entities.context: Bank holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. estate

entities.context: Bank on which Thomas R. Powers drew checks for $1350 and $2 dated November 2, 1918

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S1/2 NE, SE1/4 NW, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S., R.34E.) as Crow allottee No. 2336 under Act of April 11, 1882; trust patent issued December 2, 1907
Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent No. 227584 signed by President Calvin Coolidge on December 21, 1923
Petition for Sale of Land by Original Allottee (Frederick Geisdorff Jr.) received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Government Farmer appraised Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment at $1,194 in connection with Powers lease/sale request
Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending leasing papers and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment for sale on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale; allottee's age of under 21 was overlooked
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 accepted his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; balance of purchase price demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of two checks from Thomas R. Powers for balance of sale price ($1350 and $2 advertising fee)
Letter written to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. noting he was only 20 years of age and land was advertised for sale; doubts expressed about Indian Bureau approval; copy sent to Supt. Kneale
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote to Crow Agency stating Fritz died a few days ago of influenza
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers informing him of doubts about sale approval due to allottee's age and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Emily Lucile Geisdorff born, daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Supt. C. Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote Superintendent at Crow Agency inquiring if agency could aid her in paying note owed by her husband before his death
Superintendent answered Emily J. Geisdorff's letter regretting no funds available; noted sale pending but uncertain due to allottee's age; stated hearing would be advertised to determine heirs
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning land sale, noting allottee born May 6, 1898, holding two checks from Powers ($1350 and $150)
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner, asking if she wished pending sale completed

Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter stating nothing shows brother/sister want to sell allotments or widow wants sale to proceed; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking if she wished sale completed to Powers for $1500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority to put old sale through
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner including acceptance of widow of allottee
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Supt. Kneale at Fort Duchesne
General Land Office confirmed no reservations or withdrawals on land; no reasons why patent in fee should not be issued to purchaser; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner requesting completion of Land Sale No. 991, noting Powers requested return of deposit if nothing definite done
Fee Patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment No. 2336, signed by President Woodrow Wilson
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Crow Agency Superintendent for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signed receipt
Receipt for patent issued to Thomas R. Powers, allotment No. 2336, 159.67 acres, signed by C.H. Asbury
Crow Agency wrote to U.S. Land Office Billings Montana noting fee patent No. 719146 issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.

Crow Agency wrote to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff informing her that after heirs determined, proceeds of sale can be transferred to her agency upon request; minor's shares cannot be paid without special authority
Report on heirship and recommendations transmitted to Secretary of Interior regarding estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
Heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. determined; Emily J. Geisdorff entitled to one half plus dower interest; Emily Lucile Geisdorff entitled to balance
Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share of sale proceeds; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee

financial_transactions.description: Purchase of 159.67 acres of Crow allotment No. 2336 under Crow Land Sale No. 991; bid accepted October 24, 1918; balance paid November 5, 1918
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
Balance of purchase price for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment, check on Sheridan Banking Company dated November 2, 1918
Advertising fee for Crow Land Sale No. 991, check on Sheridan Banking Company
Heirship fee deducted from estate proceeds as authorized by Indian Office
Share of sale proceeds representing one half plus dower interest; check No. 10613
Minor daughter's share of sale proceeds, credited to her account
Lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Total amount on books to credit of Frederick Geisdorff deceased before deductions
Appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment by Government Farmer

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment

correspondence.subject: Acceptance of bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Remit balance of purchase price within 30 days and sign affidavit of vendee

correspondence.subject: Age issue and doubts about Indian Bureau approval of sale
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names of relatives; obtain statement from Supt. Kneale recommending approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received; allottee died November 27, 1918

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None - notification only
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death

correspondence.subject: Doubts about sale approval due to allottee's age; offer to cancel sale
correspondence.action_requested: Powers to request return of payment if desired; Frederick Geisdorff Jr. to submit information
correspondence.outcome: Sale eventually completed after widow's acceptance

correspondence.subject: Request for assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial assistance; pending sale status
correspondence.action_requested: None immediate; hearing to be advertised to determine heirs
correspondence.outcome: Widow informed of pending sale uncertainty and upcoming heirship hearing

correspondence.subject: Review of land sale matters for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale; allottee born May 6, 1898; holding checks from Powers for $1350 and $150

correspondence.subject: Pending sale of allotment; inquiry whether widow wishes sale completed
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to indicate whether she wishes pending sale completed

correspondence.subject: Birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff
correspondence.action_requested: None - notification only
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of minor heir

correspondence.sender: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24 letter regarding Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; otherwise reject bid and readvertise after heirs determined
correspondence.outcome: Asbury proceeded with sale papers after obtaining widow's acceptance

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment of deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wishes sale to proceed to Powers for $1500; write if she does not wish sale to go through

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991 - enclosure of sale papers with widow's acceptance
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; Powers requesting return of deposit if no action taken
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date; Powers threatening to withdraw

correspondence.subject: Copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
correspondence.action_requested: None - informational copy

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to Powers upon signed receipt; forward original receipt to Washington

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of sale and minor's share
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to request transfer of proceeds to her agency; minor's share requires special authority

correspondence.subject: Fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Notation of patent issuance

correspondence.subject: Heirship report and recommendations for estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration and approval of heirship determination

correspondence.subject: Distribution of estate proceeds
correspondence.action_requested: None - transmittal of check No. 10613 for $1,317.26
correspondence.outcome: Check sent; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act; basis for trust patent issued December 2, 1907 for Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment was made as amended
Referenced in fee patent conditions regarding water rights acquisition system
Act under which Francis Geisdorff homestead allotment was made; trust period of 25 years established
